steps.title: Read Henrys reassurance
steps.content: Henry says the play is only for amusement among themselves, with no audience and no publicity. He also says they can choose an unexceptionable play and face no greater danger than ordinary conversation.

steps.title: Choose the main purpose
steps.content: Choice B invents a changed plan. Choice C claims Henry is discussing the group's lack of skill. Choice D contradicts his statement that they want no audience or publicity. The remaining choice captures his assurance that the play will be harmless and private. The answer is A.
